Nesaea glareosa is a plant species that belongs to the family Nyctaginaceae, which is a group of flowering plants commonly known as four o'clocks or fuchsia flowers. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Nesaea glareosa include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Herbal preparations made with Nesaea glareosa include tea infusions, decoctions, and extracts, which are used to treat various health conditions, including skin infections and respiratory problems.
